name: Green Dragon Crescent Blade

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.title: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
sources.description: The primary source for the Green Dragon Crescent Blade's legend, detailing its use by Guan Yu and its significance in his battles.

sources.author: Moss Roberts (translator)
sources.title: "Three Kingdoms: A Historical Novel"
sources.description: An English translation of Luo Guanzhong's Romance of the Three Kingdoms, providing access to the story for English-speaking audiences and scholars.

longDescription:
Qinglong Yanyue Dao - The Legendary Glaive of Guan Yu The Green Dragon Crescent Blade (Chinese: Qinglong Yanyue Dao) is perhaps the most
                    famous weapon in Chinese literature and folklore, the legendary glaive wielded by
                    Guan Yu during the tumultuous Three Kingdoms period (220-280 CE). This massive
                    pole weapon has become an enduring symbol of Chinese martial tradition, loyalty,
                    and righteousness.
symbolism:
Deification of Guan Yu
                
                    Following his death, Guan Yu was gradually deified, eventually becoming known as
                    Guan Di (Emperor Guan) or Guan Gong (Lord Guan). He is worshipped as the God of
                    War, patron of soldiers and martial artists, but also paradoxically as a god of
                    literature and wealth. Temples to Guan Di exist throughout China and wherever
                    Chinese communities have settled.
                
                
                    The Green Dragon Crescent Blade appears in virtually every depiction of Guan Di,
                    becoming inseparable from his image. The weapon represents not just martial power
                    but the righteous application of force - violence in service of justice and loyalty.
                

                Martial Arts Influence
                
                    The guandao (a generic term for this type of weapon, literally "Guan's blade")
                    became a standard weapon in Chinese martial arts, inspired by Guan Yu's legendary
                    weapon. Numerous martial arts schools teach guandao techniques, and the weapon
                    appears in traditional forms and demonstrations.
                
                
                    In martial arts symbolism, the guandao represents mastery over great power. The
                    difficulty of wielding such a heavy, long weapon makes it a test of strength,
                    skill, and dedication - qualities associated with Guan Yu himself.
                

                Symbol of Righteousness
                
                    The blade has come to symbolize righteous power throughout Chinese culture. Police
                    stations, government buildings, and even businesses often display images of Guan Yu
                    with his blade as protection against evil and corruption. The weapon represents
                    justice that is strong enough to enforce itself.

extendedContent.content:
The Green Dragon Crescent Blade (Chinese: Qinglong Yanyue Dao) is perhaps the most
                    famous weapon in Chinese literature and folklore, the legendary glaive wielded by
                    Guan Yu during the tumultuous Three Kingdoms period (220-280 CE). This massive
                    pole weapon has become an enduring symbol of Chinese martial tradition, loyalty,
                    and righteousness.
                
                
                    The weapon's name derives from its distinctive crescent-shaped blade, said to
                    resemble a dragon's sweeping form. The "Green Dragon" refers both to the azure-green
                    color traditionally associated with the blade and to the dragon imagery that
                    symbolizes power, wisdom, and divine favor in Chinese culture.
                
                
                    According to the Romance of the Three Kingdoms, the blade weighed 82 jin
                    (approximately 41 kilograms or 90 pounds in modern measurements). This extraordinary
                    weight meant that only someone of Guan Yu's legendary strength could effectively
                    wield it in battle - the weapon itself became proof of his superhuman prowess.
extendedContent.title: Description and Appearance
Guan Yu and His Blade
extendedContent.content:
The Historical and Legendary Figure
                
                    Guan Yu (died 220 CE) was a general serving under the warlord Liu Bei during the
                    late Eastern Han dynasty and Three Kingdoms period. While historically documented,
                    Guan Yu's legend has grown far beyond historical records through centuries of
                    storytelling, culminating in the 14th-century novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms.
                
                
                    In both history and legend, Guan Yu is remembered as the paragon of loyalty and
                    righteousness. His bond with Liu Bei and Zhang Fei - the famous "Oath of the Peach
                    Garden" - became the model of sworn brotherhood. His refusal to betray Liu Bei
                    even when offered wealth and position by Cao Cao exemplifies the Chinese ideal
                    of yi (righteousness/loyalty).
                

                Famous Deeds with the Blade
                
                    The Romance of the Three Kingdoms describes numerous legendary feats performed by
                    Guan Yu with his Green Dragon Crescent Blade:
                
                
                    Slaying Hua Xiong: In his first major battle, Guan Yu killed
                    the feared general Hua Xiong before the wine offered to him had cooled - a feat
                    that established his reputation overnight
                    Passing Five Passes: When returning to Liu Bei's service,
                    Guan Yu fought his way through five passes, killing six generals who blocked his
                    path, all with his great blade
                    Single Combat Victories: Guan Yu defeated numerous champions
                    in single combat, with his blade cleaving through opponents and their weapons alike
                    Night Operations: The blade was said to glow with ethereal
                    light during night battles, visible across the battlefield

Deification of Guan Yu
                
                    Following his death, Guan Yu was gradually deified, eventually becoming known as
                    Guan Di (Emperor Guan) or Guan Gong (Lord Guan). He is worshipped as the God of
                    War, patron of soldiers and martial artists, but also paradoxically as a god of
                    literature and wealth. Temples to Guan Di exist throughout China and wherever
                    Chinese communities have settled.
                
                
                    The Green Dragon Crescent Blade appears in virtually every depiction of Guan Di,
                    becoming inseparable from his image. The weapon represents not just martial power
                    but the righteous application of force - violence in service of justice and loyalty.
                

                Martial Arts Influence
                
                    The guandao (a generic term for this type of weapon, literally "Guan's blade")
                    became a standard weapon in Chinese martial arts, inspired by Guan Yu's legendary
                    weapon. Numerous martial arts schools teach guandao techniques, and the weapon
                    appears in traditional forms and demonstrations.
                
                
                    In martial arts symbolism, the guandao represents mastery over great power. The
                    difficulty of wielding such a heavy, long weapon makes it a test of strength,
                    skill, and dedication - qualities associated with Guan Yu himself.
                

                Symbol of Righteousness
                
                    The blade has come to symbolize righteous power throughout Chinese culture. Police
                    stations, government buildings, and even businesses often display images of Guan Yu
                    with his blade as protection against evil and corruption. The weapon represents
                    justice that is strong enough to enforce itself.
extendedContent.title: Symbolism and Cultural Impact
Modern Depictions
extendedContent.content:
Film and Television
                
                    Red Cliff (2008-2009): Features Guan Yu wielding the blade in
                    spectacular battle sequences
                    Numerous Three Kingdoms Adaptations: Every film and TV version
                    prominently features Guan Yu and his iconic weapon
                    Dynasty Warriors Series: The video game franchise features
                    Guan Yu as a playable character with his signature weapon
                

                Video Games
                
                    Dynasty Warriors/Warriors Orochi: Guan Yu is a central character
                    SMITE: Features Guan Yu as a playable god
                    Total War: Three Kingdoms: Guan Yu appears as a legendary general
                    Honor of Kings: Popular Chinese mobile game featuring Guan Yu
                

                Cultural Presence
                
                    Statues of Guan Yu with his blade stand throughout the Chinese-speaking world.
                    The largest, in Jingzhou, China, stands 58 meters tall. The weapon continues to
                    be a popular subject for traditional craftsmen, martial arts practitioners, and
                    modern artists alike.

cultural.modernLegacy: The Green Dragon Crescent Blade remains a potent symbol in Chinese culture, representing courage, loyalty, and martial prowess. It frequently appears in video games, films, and literature, solidifying Guan Yu's status as a revered figure and the blade as an iconic weapon. The blade is often depicted in martial arts demonstrations and is a popular motif in tattoos and artwork, signifying strength and honor.

shortDescription: Qinglong Yanyue Dao - The Legendary Glaive of Guan Yu
description:
The Green Dragon Crescent Blade, known in Chinese as the Qinglong Yanyue Dao (青龍偃月刀), is a legendary glaive wielded by the esteemed general Guan Yu from the historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms. Described as a guandao, it is distinguished by its immense size and weight, said to be around 82 kilograms (180 pounds). The blade's name derives from the intricate design of a green dragon adorning its surface and its crescent-shaped blade. It is not just a weapon but an emblem of Guan Yu's unparalleled martial skill, unwavering loyalty, and righteous character.

According to legend, the Green Dragon Crescent Blade was forged from the heavens, imbued with the spirit of a dragon. Its immense weight and razor-sharp edge made it a fearsome weapon in battle, capable of cleaving through enemy ranks with ease. The blade became synonymous with Guan Yu's exploits, contributing significantly to his deification as a symbol of loyalty and righteousness. Its association with Guan Yu has elevated the blade to a symbol of immense cultural significance.

The Green Dragon Crescent Blade is more than just a weapon; it is a symbol of virtue, strength, and unwavering commitment. Its image continues to inspire artists, writers, and martial arts practitioners, solidifying its place in Chinese folklore and popular culture. The blade serves as a constant reminder of the values that Guan Yu embodied, making it a potent symbol for generations to come.

associations.name: Green
associations.significance: Represents spring, vitality, and growth, aligning with Guan Yu's righteousness and flourishing spirit.

associations.name: Dragon
associations.significance: Symbolizes power, strength, and imperial authority, reflecting the blade's formidable nature and Guan Yu's unmatched prowess.

associations.name: Crescent Moon
associations.significance: Represents change, cycles, and the waxing and waning of fortune, alluding to the unpredictable nature of battle and destiny.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: The blade is strongly associated with Guan Yu's unwavering loyalty to his sworn brothers, Liu Bei and Zhang Fei.

associations.name: Metal
associations.significance: Represents strength, resilience, and the ability to cut through obstacles, mirroring the blade's sharpness and Guan Yu's determination.

associations.name: Autumn
associations.significance: While green is associated with spring, the blade's association with warfare and heroic death connects it to the harvest season and the end of life's cycle.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: A legendary spear wielded by Odin, symbolizing authority and martial power, similar to the Green Dragon Crescent Blade's association with Guan Yu's strength and leadership.
A sword of immense power and symbolic importance, tied to the legitimacy and strength of King Arthur, paralleling the Green Dragon Crescent Blade's connection to Guan Yu's legendary status.
The shield of Zeus, often associated with protection and divine authority, shares the Green Dragon Crescent Blade's aura of invincibility and its wielder's unmatched skill.

subtitle: The Legendary Glaive of Guan Yu, Symbol of Loyalty

wielders.name: Guan Yu
wielders.context: The primary and most famous wielder of the Green Dragon Crescent Blade, known for his loyalty and martial prowess.

wielders.name: Zhou Cang
wielders.context: A loyal follower of Guan Yu who sometimes carried the blade for him due to its immense weight.
